name: cappy
description: AI planning assistant for VS Code — creates structured task plans through conversation.

commands.title: Cappy: Test LLM (envia Olá para a LLM)

cappy.llm.preferredModel.description: Preferred language model for Cappy agents (auto = best available)
enumDescriptions: Automatically select the best available model

cappy.provider.baseUrl.description: Base URL para API OpenAI-compatible usada pelo painel Cappy.

cappy.provider.backend.description: Backend do agente: OpenAI-compatible ou OpenClaude externo.

cappy.openclaude.command.description: Comando do OpenClaude externo (usado quando backend=openclaude).

cappy.features.enableChatPanel.description: Habilita o painel de chat Planning + Sandbox.

cappy.features.enableSandbox.description: Habilita execução no modo sandbox via worktree.

cappy.features.enableOpenClaudeFallback.description: Permite fallback para provider OpenAI-compatible quando OpenClaude falhar.

chatParticipants.name: cappy
chatParticipants.description: Cappy planning assistant: asks clarifying questions and creates structured task plans (does not execute code directly)

commands.name: help
commands.description: Get help on how to use @cappy

languageModelTools.name: cappy_create_file
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Creates a new file in the workspace with the specified content. Use this when the user asks to create a file.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Create a new file in the workspace

path.description: Relative path from workspace root where the file should be created

content.description: Content to write to the file

languageModelTools.name: cappy_fetch_web
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Fetches content from a web URL (HTML, JSON, or text). Provide the url and optionally a CSS selector.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Fetch content from a web URL

selector.description: Optional CSS selector to extract specific content from HTML

languageModelTools.name: cappy_grep_search
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Search for text content in workspace files using regex or plain text. Use this to find specific code patterns, function names, or text strings.

query.description: Text or regex pattern to search for

isRegex.description: Whether the query is a regex pattern (default: false)

includePattern.description: Glob pattern for files to include (e.g., '*.ts')

languageModelTools.name: cappy_read_file
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Read the contents of a file in the workspace. Use this after finding files to understand their implementation.

filePath.description: Absolute or relative path to the file

languageModelTools.name: cappy_create_task_file
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Creates a new task file in .cappy/tasks/ with structured XML format. Use this when the user wants to create a task, feature, bugfix, or any type of development work item. The task file includes a checklist, acceptance criteria, and implementation steps.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Create a new task file for tracking development work

title.description: Title of the task (e.g., 'Implement JWT authentication', 'Fix login bug')

category.description: Type of task: feature (new functionality), bugfix (fix existing issue), refactor (code improvement), docs (documentation), test (testing), chore (maintenance)

description.description: Optional detailed description of what needs to be done

languageModelTools.name: cappy_run_terminal_command
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Executes a shell command in the workspace root. Use this to run commands like 'find', 'grep', 'ls', 'cat' to explore the workspace or search for files. Returns stdout and stderr.

command.description: The shell command to execute (e.g., 'find src -name "*.ts"', 'grep -r "TODO" .')

languageModelTools.name: cappy_check_task_file
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Checks whether a task file exists under .cappy/tasks. Useful to verify if a task was already created.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Check if a task file exists

fileName.description: File name inside .cappy/tasks (e.g., task_2025-12-21_123.ACTIVE.xml). If no extension is provided, .ACTIVE.xml and .xml will be tried.

languageModelTools.name: cappy_create_todo
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Creates a new todo item. Use this when the user wants to create a task, add something to their todo list, or remember something to do.

title.description: Title of the todo (e.g., 'Buy groceries', 'Review PR #123')

description.description: Optional detailed description of the todo

languageModelTools.name: cappy_list_todos
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Lists all todo items with their status. Use this when the user asks to see their todos, check what tasks they have, or review their todo list.

languageModelTools.name: cappy_complete_todo
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Marks a todo as completed. Use this when the user indicates they finished a task or want to mark something as done.

id.description: ID of the todo to mark as complete

scripts.package: npm version patch && npm run compile-extension && vsce package --dependencies
scripts.publish: npm run package && vsce publish && code --install-extension cappy-*.vsix
